core_payload.content:
# Hybrid LLM Access

## 1. Role
Hybrid LLM Access is the governed intelligence supply fabric of N1Hub, routing subscription seats, API pools, hosted inference, and bridge providers through one policy engine instead of treating them as unrelated access modes.

## 2. Supply Contract
The wallet and control plane understand workload classes, failover preferences, assignment types, budget posture, and safety boundaries across the full supply portfolio. Interactive symbiosis, unattended mining, and background execution therefore choose the right lane without ad hoc manual reconfiguration.

## 3. Operational Consequence
N1Hub treats access to intelligence like a resilient infrastructure portfolio rather than a provider toggle. Supply routing becomes more durable, more economical, and easier to supervise as providers and pricing models change.

## 4. Philosophical Alignment
Hybrid LLM Access supports To Dig Deep by making access diversity governable instead of accidental.

neuro_concentrate.summary: Hybrid LLM Access defines the governed intelligence supply fabric that routes subscription seats, API pools, hosted inference, and bridge providers through one policy engine. It covers workload-aware placement, failover behavior, assignment classes, budget posture, and safety boundaries across the full supply portfolio. The capsule matters because serious autonomy cannot rely on ad hoc provider toggles or manual lane switching. It turns model access into resilient infrastructure that remains economical, supervised, and adaptable as providers, pricing, and runtime demands change.

epistemic_ledger.rationale: Removed meta-language so Hybrid LLM Access states present supply doctrine directly.
